109135.explanation: Women are often very good administrators. In the past council term, GroenLinks provided two female alderwomen who achieved much for the city. It would be good if a new college consists of half (or more) women.

109136.explanation: The referendum on parking policy has led to much dissatisfaction and division. Many residents feel unheard despite adjustments to the original plan. That is why we ask the randomly selected citizens' council how a referendum can better enable residents to participate in decision-making.

109139.explanation: GL-PvdA supports a trial to see if flexible opening hours can reduce nightlife nuisance for local residents. Many problems arise because bars currently close at the same time.

109141.explanation: Rejected asylum seekers have an obligation to leave. Sometimes asylum seekers are rejected while their country of origin does not accept their return. GL-PvdA does not want these people to sleep on the streets as long as no solution has been found.

109142.explanation: Not everyone is aware of the health damage caused by wood smoke, and not everyone dares to state how much wood smoke from neighbors bothers them. This is not something people easily resolve together. Therefore, municipal regulations are necessary.

109149.explanation: A good school needs a good building where children can stay healthy. Priority must be given to buildings in the worst condition.

109154.explanation: Camera surveillance gives a false sense of security if images cannot be viewed live. We therefore prefer more enforcement officers on the street who can intervene immediately if something goes wrong. This also prevents unnecessary infringement on our residents' privacy.

109156.explanation: There are no signs that enforcement officers in Amersfoort need this.

109159.explanation: The buying up of homes by investors should be countered as much as possible to the benefit of people who really need a home to live in.

109160.explanation: Where there are opportunities, the municipality should help transform office space that is long-term vacant into housing, for example if this can create more social rental homes. This way, we better use the scarce space we have for all those looking for a home.

109161.explanation: Although we prefer to focus on homes that can stand for decades to come, temporary housing can offer a short-term solution, for example for students.

109163.explanation: Pushing people further into debt ultimately costs society more—not only in money such as benefits, shelters, and (youth) care, but also in wasted talents of people who are too busy just keeping their heads above water.

109164.explanation: GL-PvdA wants to locally determine what a person in Amersfoort minimally needs to live with dignity and use this as a basis for our minimum schemes, services, and communication.

109166.explanation: Thanks in part to GL-PvdA, a successful pilot has already been conducted in Amersfoort, which we want to continue. Vulnerable youth can receive a basic income for a year via the Bouwdepot to build their lives.

109168.explanation: Amersfoort is getting busier; we must distribute the limited space we have as well as possible. We want to encourage people who can reach the shopping center by bus, bike, or on foot to do so as much as possible. This also creates more space for those who really need a car.

109171.explanation: The property tax is needed to pay for amenities in our city, such as (youth) care, poverty policy, housing construction, safety, and maintenance of public space. If we lower the property tax, cuts will have to be made here.

109172.explanation: The revenue from the dog tax is needed to cover expenses necessary to make Amersfoort a pleasant city for dogs. For example, the revenue is used for (maintenance of) dog parks and dog waste bins.

109174.explanation: As far as GLPvdA is concerned, Amersfoort has sufficient business park space. We prefer to use the limited remaining space in our city for nature or housing construction.

109176.explanation: If refugees do not have priority status along with other groups we consider important to quickly find housing, the pressure on asylum reception will increase further and it will take longer before refugees can start (re)building their lives.

109179.explanation: Since the abolition of school swimming lessons in 2012, it has been the responsibility of parents to arrange swimming lessons for their children, not the schools. GLPvdA does find it important to support parents with less money in being able to afford swimming lessons for their children.

109180.explanation: Amersfoort currently has seven coffeeshop licenses. GLPvdA finds it important that there is sufficient supply so that soft drug trade does not move to the streets.

109181.explanation: Partly due to our efforts, it has been recorded that at least 35% of new construction must be social rental housing. However, raising that percentage even higher endangers the feasibility of housing projects, which risks leading to fewer social rental homes overall.

109187.explanation: We want to be mindful of tight situations. However, Amersfoort is getting busier. Therefore, we want to encourage people who can travel by public transport, bicycle, or on foot to do so as much as possible. This leaves more space for those who depend on cars.

109188.explanation: GLPvdA wants good housing for Flint, Fluor, and De Lieve Vrouw. Whether the old city hall location is suitable and can be realized affordably is currently being investigated. We would like to see not only a center but also new homes built on the city hall site.

109189.explanation: On warm days, there is a shortage of public swimming water in our city. If there are opportunities to practically and affordably realize additional swimming water, GLPvdA is in favor.

109191.explanation: GLPvdA chooses a realistic and sustainable traffic solution, focusing primarily on pedestrians, cyclists, and public transport. We seek a solution that ensures safety, improves traffic flow, and fits our vision of sustainable mobility. An automobile tunnel is not our priority.

109192.explanation: We want residents who produce less waste to pay less tax.

109193.explanation: To combat global warming and reduce dependency on gas-producing countries, we must generate more sustainable energy faster. GLPvdA therefore wants to proceed with the development of already planned wind turbines, and smaller turbines on suitable corner locations on existing buildings.

109194.explanation: The current station environments are old and paved over. We want to green these areas, make them more attractive, and increase their social safety. This will make using public transport more pleasant.

109195.explanation: It is a matter of civilization to provide the most vulnerable in the city with a roof over their heads.

109135.explanation: For D66, diversity is important. We therefore want an equal distribution of men and women in municipal governance.

109136.explanation: In a referendum, residents can only answer yes or no to a question. Important topics require more nuance. Other forms of participation are then more appropriate, such as a randomly selected citizens' council, city conversations, or a survey.

109139.explanation: D66 supports longer opening hours for hospitality venues. Where this does not cause additional nuisance, D66 supports enabling 24-hour opening for hospitality.

109141.explanation: Rejected asylum seekers return to the country they came from.

109142.explanation: Clean air is important. Wood smoke during bad weather causes nuisance, especially for people with asthma or other lung conditions.

109149.explanation: Amersfoort has long-term agreements with school boards about new construction and renovation of school buildings. Money will also be invested in this during the coming council period.

109154.explanation: D66 places the privacy of residents central. We only deploy camera surveillance if no other effective measures are possible and there is a demonstrably significant societal interest.

109156.explanation: Carrying a baton belongs to the police. The police are trained and equipped for this. Better training and prevention for enforcement officers (BOAs) contribute more to street safety.

109159.explanation: There is a housing shortage in Amersfoort. This measure prevents homes from being bought up for the (expensive) rental sector.

109160.explanation: D66 focuses on a city where entrepreneurship is encouraged. Therefore, we want to create experimental space where entrepreneurs can discuss with the municipality the conditions under which they can be given more space on a pilot basis.

109161.explanation: Students must be able to live affordably, but there is a great shortage of affordable student housing. D66 wants the municipality to make room again for student houses, including in the city center.

109163.explanation: Everyone must be able to participate. Therefore, we want good income arrangements with tailored solutions where possible and equal opportunities for everyone.

109164.explanation: Everyone must be able to participate. Therefore, we want good income arrangements with tailored solutions where possible and equal opportunities for everyone. Poverty and debt cause stress, health problems, and social exclusion.

109166.explanation: D66 wants to move towards a basic amount for everyone in the long term. This will allow us to do away with the complicated benefits system in which people get stuck, providing more certainty and simplicity for real social security. A pilot for this in Amersfoort would be a good first step.

109168.explanation: Amersfoort has introduced paid parking at shopping centers (€0.30 per hour for 2 hours), so only visitors to the shopping center park there. This ensures the shopping center is easily accessible and there is sufficient parking without adding extra parking spaces.

109171.explanation: D66 aims to keep the property tax (ozb) from rising more than inflation. Steeper increases are only applied if strictly necessary to keep the municipality financially healthy.

109172.explanation: D66 supports the polluter pays principle. This also applies to dog owners.

109174.explanation: There is a great shortage of business parks in the region for practical professions, manufacturing, and the circular economy. Therefore, business parks are preserved for this activity, and space is created for innovative companies and initiatives for sustainability, reuse, and circularity.

109176.explanation: Many groups are entitled to urgency status, including status holders. We can only meet the national government’s allocation by also considering status holders as an urgency group.

109179.explanation: D66 ensures that sufficient swimming lessons and swimming opportunities remain available in the city, and that swimming lessons are accessible to all residents. This does not therefore have to be organized through schools.

109180.explanation: The number of coffeeshops should match demand, and locations must be far from schools. D66 wants more attention for safe drug use, including testing locations at events and in nightlife.

109181.explanation: For many Amersfoort residents, a social rental home is not suitable and rental or homeownership in the free sector is too expensive. 35% of new homes in Amersfoort are social rental homes. Therefore, more mid-price owner-occupied and rental homes should be built.

109187.explanation: Paid parking has been introduced to keep our city accessible and liveable. We have ensured low costs for the first car, good arrangements for caregivers, and low costs for visitors. Moreover, this encourages people to travel by bike or public transport.

109188.explanation: Flint, Lieve Vrouw, and Fluor want a new location so Amersfoort can have a larger and future-proof cultural venue. D66 supports this. The current buildings are outdated, not sustainable, and expensive to maintain. A new center offers residents, visitors, and artists a broader cultural offering.

109189.explanation: D66 finds it important that sufficient places for swimming lessons and swimming opportunities remain available in Amersfoort. This can be at locations other than a public swimming pond.

109191.explanation: D66 is making way for cyclists. Therefore, we focus on more through-cycle routes. These are routes specifically designed for cycling, with extra priority for cyclists or wide cycling lanes. Also, a bicycle tunnel at the Barchman Wuytierslaan railway crossing.

109192.explanation: D66 wants to stimulate the reuse of raw materials. Waste contains raw materials that can be reused. We ensure the polluter pays by encouraging reuse of raw materials from waste with a price incentive.

109193.explanation: Energy from wind complements solar energy and makes Amersfoort more energy independent. D66 therefore asks the municipality to continue the plans to build two wind turbines on the Isselt.

109194.explanation: For Amersfoort's growth towards 2050, it is necessary that Amersfoort Centraal and Schothorst stations also grow. The stations need to be renewed and expanded. Only in this way can we continue to serve all travelers and be ready for the future.

109195.explanation: No one sleeps on the street. D66 focuses on preventing homelessness through good collaboration among professionals. There must be enough shelter, linked to a sustainable reintegration trajectory. Because a familiar environment contributes to recovery, progressing to independence is important.

109135.explanation: Desirable, but not always feasible. CDA Amersfoort values diversity. However, quality and suitability are leading for the CDA.

109136.explanation: Amersfoort residents must be able to participate in major decisions. That is why CDA Amersfoort voted in favor of the referendum regulation.

109139.explanation: We want lively hospitality venues and quiet residential streets. For the livability and safety of the area, it is undesirable that hospitality establishments can set their own opening hours.

109141.explanation: Rejected asylum seekers who do not cooperate with their return cannot remain in Amersfoort.

109142.explanation: Wood-burning stoves are harmful to health and air quality.

109149.explanation: Buildings already have a multi-year maintenance plan. We actively green schoolyards with natural shaded areas, green roofs, green facades, and space to move.

109154.explanation: Camera surveillance works preventively, contributes to the feeling of safety, and supports the detection of offenders. The potential benefits clearly outweigh the limited intrusion on privacy.

109156.explanation: BOAs sometimes face risky situations. CDA Amersfoort wants them to have appropriate means. The condition is that they receive proper training to prevent unnecessary escalation.

109159.explanation: We have a shortage of homes. Houses are for living in, not for speculation.

109160.explanation: Long-term vacant offices can better be converted into homes. CDA Amersfoort supports this but believes subsidies for this should be arranged nationally or regionally.

109161.explanation: Students and young people face extra difficulties on the housing market. CDA Amersfoort supports temporary and affordable student housing as part of the approach to the housing shortage.

109163.explanation: Customization on this point is existing policy.

109164.explanation: In Amersfoort, various schemes are already available at 120% to 150% of the minimum wage.

109166.explanation: A basic income is a national issue. Therefore, CDA Amersfoort believes it belongs at the national level.

109168.explanation: Parking spaces are being used differently by people who are not shopping and who park their cars for long periods.

109171.explanation: CDA Amersfoort is careful with tax money. From stewardship and municipal finances perspective, a property tax reduction is not justifiable at this time.

109172.explanation: From the perspective of stewardship and municipal income, CDA Amersfoort supports abolition, but as long as dog tax remains, the money must clearly go back to good facilities for dogs.

109174.explanation: Amersfoort has a shortage of land. Space is scarce and we want to build houses and preserve land.

109176.explanation: It is a legal task to help status holders find housing. There are other focus groups and urgent cases that also need help.

109179.explanation: CDA Amersfoort believes every child should have the opportunity to obtain a swimming diploma through school swimming, for the safety of every child.

109180.explanation: Amersfoort already has enough of it. We want to protect the youth.

109181.explanation: Amersfoort already has a policy with 35% social housing. At a higher percentage, CDA Amersfoort foresees that the total number of houses built will be lower.

109187.explanation: For accessibility and the construction of thousands of homes near the center, permit parking must remain in and around the inner city. CDA Amersfoort sees no need for further expansion of permit parking.

109188.explanation: Yes, in combination with housing on the current locations of Flint, theater de Lieve Vrouw, and possibly at the current city hall.

109189.explanation: Amersfoort has a great need for open swimming water.

109191.explanation: The responsibility lies with ProRail. CDA Amersfoort finds the safety of cyclists and pedestrians very important and wants a grade-separated crossing for them.

109192.explanation: CDA Amersfoort is against Diftar, paying for waste based on amount.

109193.explanation: CDA Amersfoort supports wind energy, especially when generated in polders and at sea. In the densely populated Amersfoort region, wind energy is only possible within the frameworks of laws and regulations, after good participation, and broad support from residents in the immediate vicinity.

109194.explanation: It is primarily a task for ProRail. Our three stations (Amersfoort Centraal, Schothorst and Vathorst) are the gateways to the city and all three deserve a facelift.

109195.explanation: CDA Amersfoort wants no one to have to sleep on the street at night. This shelter has emerged from the procurement, and we commit to a suitable location.

109135.explanation: It is good for recognizability if backgrounds, life experiences, beliefs and characteristics of council members vary. The nomination is the outcome of a democratic process and therefore in the hands of the elected parties. We do not want an obligation that implies this position.

109136.explanation: For us, democracy is a verb. The core is that elected representatives make decisions after a good debate. Therefore, we are cautious with citizen assemblies and referenda: political choices involve weighing values and interests. This requires open debate and accountability.

109139.explanation: The city is a community in which we consider each other so that every Amersfoorter can live a good life. In the municipal council, we set frameworks on subjects such as closing times, based on the general interest.

109141.explanation: Of course there are limits to what a city and community can handle, but we are morally and legally obliged to offer hospitality to people who seek refuge with us within the framework of Dutch law.

109142.explanation: ChristenUnie wants everyone in Amersfoort to be able to breathe healthy air. A complicated heating ban with certain color codes does not work in practice. Therefore, we promote the use of filters. These remove most of the nuisance. This way, we protect health without unnecessary bans.

109149.explanation: We assess the construction plans for school buildings of Samenfoort PO and Samenvoort VO based on criteria related to health, safety and functionality such as sufficient ventilation, accessibility, and green schoolyards.

109154.explanation: We provide better street lighting, camera surveillance at risk locations, and more visible police presence in the neighborhood.

109156.explanation: We provide our enforcement officers with the resources to intervene where necessary.

109159.explanation: We support an owner-occupation obligation to ensure homes are available for our residents.

109160.explanation: We choose active land policy: the municipality takes a leading role in acquiring land and properties, so we can genuinely steer affordability, sustainability, and social functions. For example, by purchasing vacated office buildings.

109161.explanation: An action plan for youth housing will be developed, for instance by deploying flexible housing units.

109163.explanation: We apply a social debt relief and collection policy when collecting municipal taxes and levies to prevent problematic debts.

109168.explanation: The first two hours of parking at shopping centers should be free.

109171.explanation: Amersfoort already has relatively high local taxes and currently has a budget surplus.

109172.explanation: We actively address street litter and dog poop, because small annoyances have a big impact on living enjoyment.

109174.explanation: Employment grows along with the population. Together with the region, we create space for manufacturing, circular businesses, and recycling, for example through a regional business park. In neighborhoods, we make room for small businesses and self-employed people that mix well with the neighborhood.

109176.explanation: There are more priority groups, including refugees with residence permits (status holders).

109179.explanation: Every child in Amersfoort should be able to swim by the end of primary school. We want to promote this specifically through sufficient resources and awareness of the Sports Fund available for families with low incomes.

109180.explanation: No new coffeeshops will be established in Amersfoort. We implement a restrictive permit policy and use every permit revision as an opportunity to reduce the number of coffeeshops.

109181.explanation: The agreed norm of 35% social rental housing in new construction remains a starting point for new development projects.

109187.explanation: Parking is free in much of the city, and it is up to residents in the neighborhood to decide whether paid parking is necessary. In certain neighborhoods, it certainly is.

109188.explanation: We are open to relocating the Flint and other cultural venues to the current city hall location, but we do not see a need to scale up our cultural offerings. The location must be affordable and focused on meeting places, for example for clubs, schools, and churches.

109189.explanation: We focus on official, safe, and accessible places to swim or play in and near the water, in close cooperation with the water board, province, and local associations. Cleaner water offers opportunities for events like a City Swim.

109191.explanation: We will investigate whether a grade-separated railway crossing at Barchman Wuytierslaan can help reduce traffic pressure on the City Ring and Amsterdam Road, making it feasible to redesign these roads. The expansion of Defense also makes this study necessary!

109192.explanation: This term we do not choose to charge for waste by the kilo (diftar) because it disproportionately affects families and is therefore not solidary. We will continue to focus on encouraging waste reduction.

109193.explanation: Wind turbines are necessary to realize the energy transition. We see possibilities for this in Amersfoort under the following conditions: they are safe, do not harm health, there is sufficient support, and residents are partly owners and benefit from the revenues.

109194.explanation: The city is growing, so public transport must be an attractive alternative to the car. These stations are reaching their limits. Larger stations also make it more attractive for NS to keep Amersfoort on important routes as a station.

109195.explanation: We are in the middle of a participation process.

109135.explanation: The VVD aims for a reflection of society in which qualities matter more than gender.

109136.explanation: In Amersfoort, residents have many opportunities to express their opinions. A referendum is one of these ways. Unfortunately, a referendum lacks dialogue, and the VVD wants to conduct that dialogue. The decision lies with the municipal council, as representative of the people on behalf of the VVD.

109139.explanation: Extended opening hours provide more space for entrepreneurship, and spreading opening times usually has a positive effect on potential disturbances.

109141.explanation: Once their procedure has been completed, it is important that asylum seekers actually return to their country of origin.

109142.explanation: Nuisance caused by wood burning is basically a matter between neighbors. The municipality should not take sides or act proactively. Enforcement of 'prohibited wood burning' is difficult and time-consuming.

109149.explanation: The VVD believes every child should receive education in a safe school building with sufficient ventilation and good heating. The responsibility for the buildings lies primarily with the school boards. Together with the municipality, we ensure optimal school buildings.

109154.explanation: If applied carefully, camera surveillance has a positive contribution to safety on the streets. Furthermore, the feeling of safety appears to increase due to camera surveillance.

109156.explanation: Enforcement officers (BOAs) must be given the proper equipment to perform their work. Unfortunately, a baton is necessary. Additionally, the VVD would also like every BOA to have a body camera. These sometimes prove more effective than a baton.

109159.explanation: If you rent out your home to residents and do not live there yourself, you contribute to the housing market. And rental homes are also desperately needed. Moreover, the VVD believes it is important that owners are free to decide what they do with their property.

109160.explanation: Utilizing existing spaces is important for housing shortages. So converting offices into homes remains important. However, we must continue to balance the need for offices and homes. Every project should be assessed individually.

109161.explanation: The space for building, temporary or otherwise, is very limited. This applies to every starter and student. Moreover, it postpones a problem: when temporary housing ends, where will the student live? Together with housing corporations, we look for creative solutions, including more room rentals.

109163.explanation: Every situation is different and should be considered as such. Additionally, everyone with a low income should contribute to municipal charges according to their capacity to pay.

109164.explanation: Municipal arrangements must align well with reality. The VVD believes work must pay off. However, sometimes when someone works more, they lose all allowances, which has the opposite effect.

109166.explanation: This is difficult to organize locally. A pilot should be organized nationally and in consultation with a municipality.

109168.explanation: Visiting shopping centers is vital for visitors and shopkeepers. Parking can be regulated with a blue parking card if needed and possibly at the request of the shopkeepers.

109171.explanation: The property tax (ozb) must be balanced with other taxes so that owners do not pay disproportionately for general services.

109172.explanation: As long as dog waste bins and dog poop bags are provided to keep public spaces clean. Once this stops, the dog tax should also stop.

109174.explanation: Business activity is the engine of society. People need to be able to work nearby, so preferably expand with companies that require many people per square meter.

109176.explanation: The Netherlands and Amersfoort face a housing shortage. The average waiting time for social housing is now over 8 years. Too many groups currently have urgency compared to others. These rules need to be limited. This means status holders will have to live longer in asylum seeker centers.

109179.explanation: Not every family sees the necessity of swimming lessons. Information and possibly financial support have a positive effect on swimming skills and thus on a child's safety.

109180.explanation: The current number of coffeeshops seems to meet the demand.

109181.explanation: In Amersfoort, the share of social rental housing has not exceeded 29% in the past 15 years despite the 35% social housing requirement. New construction projects stall if the requirement becomes 40%. Building for specific target groups (seniors, starters, etc.) and affordable homes is more effective than the 40% requirement.

109187.explanation: Buying parking permits where there is no parking problem in the neighborhood is unnecessary. Parking regulation must be customized per neighborhood. If there is a parking problem in the neighborhood, permit policy or a blue zone can help. No parking problem? Then do not enforce parking permits.

109188.explanation: The VVD is brief here; there is more need for housing.

109191.explanation: The Langs Eem and Spoort construction project will provide thousands of new homes. Those residents, as well as emergency services, will also use the Barchman Wuytierslaan. The standstill caused by train traffic will cause unnecessary congestion. A car tunnel will help. Also if Ring Amersfoort narrows.

109192.explanation: Residents and businesses can be encouraged to produce less (residual) waste. However, charging at the recycling center encourages illegal dumping.

109193.explanation: Onshore wind turbines have limited yield and cause much nuisance to the immediate surroundings in terms of noise, visibility, and shadow flicker. Birds and bats also suffer greatly from wind turbines. The VVD has been calling for years: no wind turbines within the municipality of Amersfoort.

109194.explanation: The stations themselves are managed by ProRail. However, the municipality can ensure a good environment so residents can use the station efficiently and safely.

109195.explanation: The municipality has a role in sheltering the homeless. But the choice of location at Bergstraat is insufficiently substantiated. Furthermore, the shelter also offers day care (regional), and this is highly undesirable in this location, in the midst of a residential neighborhood.

109135.explanation: Quality comes first and is most important to us. Same for the number of aldermen: a maximum of six. Diversity is desirable but is not solely dependent on gender.

109136.explanation: This possibility exists through a valid regulation, which we also agree with. However, when participation on the most important issues goes well, a referendum should not be necessary. When residents do want this, we believe that is their right.

109139.explanation: Some regulation is needed to prevent nuisance.

109141.explanation: Amersfoort2014 believes that the legal rules as established by the national government in the Aliens Act apply.

109142.explanation: Amersfoort2014 believes that wood burning must remain prohibited during code red (air quality).

109149.explanation: Amersfoort2014 already supports the ongoing initiative to improve school housing in Amersfoort, though this is behind schedule. There is already budget available.

109154.explanation: Amersfoort2014 believes there should be more camera surveillance but only in places where it is truly needed to increase safety.

109156.explanation: Amersfoort2014 believes this should be enabled for specific situations, and public order officers must be trained for this.

109159.explanation: Amersfoort2014 believes that homes are meant to live in, not to speculate with.

109160.explanation: Amersfoort2014 believes that, in some cases, an extra investment by the municipality should be possible to stimulate the construction of social housing.

109161.explanation: Amersfoort2014 believes this should be among the possibilities.

109163.explanation: Amersfoort2014 believes that current policy should continue. Residents can apply for exemption from municipal taxes.

109164.explanation: Amersfoort2014 believes that, in principle, the national poverty line calculated by CBS, CPB, and Nibud is leading. Additionally, subsidies and/or municipal schemes are available to provide tailored support where necessary.

109166.explanation: Amersfoort2014 believes this is not a municipal task. You cannot simply do this.

109168.explanation: Amersfoort2014 believes that paid parking—and for residents a parking permit—should only be introduced where there is an actual problem, or there is a clear request from residents or shopkeepers.

109171.explanation: Amersfoort2014 considers this a populist statement. The property tax (ozb) can be indexed.

109172.explanation: Amersfoort2014 believes this should be cost-covering for the facilities provided.

109174.explanation: Amersfoort2014 believes this should be considered case by case / application by application.

109176.explanation: Amersfoort2014 believes the current policy should be maintained.

109179.explanation: Amersfoort2014 believes children below the poverty line can apply for a subsidy for swimming lessons.

109180.explanation: Amersfoort2014 does not want an expansion of the number of coffeeshops in the city center and only allows new coffeeshops at a large distance from schools and preferably on industrial sites/business parks.

109181.explanation: Amersfoort2014 did vote in favor on December 16, 2025.

109187.explanation: Amersfoort2014 believes that parking should be free again where possible.

109188.explanation: Amersfoort2014 believes there should also be consideration of what Amersfoort residents themselves think the city needs in this location.

109189.explanation: Amersfoort2014 believes there should be more public outdoor water for swimming.

109191.explanation: Amersfoort2014 believes there is no need for this. If it should be needed, the question is whether Amersfoort should pay for it.

109192.explanation: Amersfoort2014 believes this is not a goal in itself; the challenge is to keep the overall costs of the waste chain manageable.

109193.explanation: Amersfoort2014 is cautious about installing wind turbines. Safety and health come first.

109194.explanation: Amersfoort2014 believes we should invest more in public transport and in the surrounding areas to make public transport more accessible and safer.

109195.explanation: Amersfoort2014 feels that participation with residents has gone well, but the ongoing legal procedure with one of the owners must first be concluded.

109135.explanation: The municipal government must be as inclusive and diverse as possible and a good reflection of society. This goes beyond the male/female division. We understand this depends on the available candidates.

109136.explanation: Residents should more easily be able to give their opinions on important issues. This can be done through a referendum, but also via a randomly selected citizens' council. This council consists of randomly chosen residents who study and think along from their own experience.

109139.explanation: If cafes and restaurants set their own opening hours, this can lead to more nuisance. Residents must be able to live and sleep peacefully. Staggering closing times can sometimes help, but the municipality must make rules to maintain control and limit nuisance.

109141.explanation: No one should sleep on the streets. Amersfoort must offer a bed-bath-bread arrangement. This also includes legal assistance. This protects the rights of people who have been wrongly denied procedures.

109142.explanation: Amersfoort continues to oppose wood burning. Wood smoke produces a lot of fine particles and causes health problems for many residents. Therefore, Amersfoort continues to inform residents about the disadvantages of wood burning and reporting smoke nuisance via the 'Stookwijzer'.

109149.explanation: A healthy indoor climate is important for students and teachers. Old schools will be renovated sustainably and nature-inclusive. New buildings will be constructed to be circular and energy-positive.

109154.explanation: Camera surveillance often gives a false sense of security and shifts problems elsewhere. Privacy is important. Only in exceptional cases with prolonged serious nuisance, such as at the Meridiaan or nightlife centers, can it be used (temporarily) while working on a permanent solution.

109156.explanation: No, enforcement officers (BOAs) do not need batons. BOAs are there to help and supervise; the use of force belongs to the police.

109159.explanation: Anyone buying a home in Amersfoort, Hoogland, or Hooglanderveen must live in it themselves. This prevents investors from buying houses and leaving them empty. Amersfoort will continue measures such as owner-occupancy obligations and purchase protection.

109160.explanation: Vacant offices must be converted into homes. If this is not possible for financial reasons, the municipality will provide subsidies to encourage housing construction.

109161.explanation: All young people urgently need housing, not just students. Flexible housing can help temporarily, but only if there is a prospect of permanent housing. We want to prevent young people from being homeless after a temporary contract.

109163.explanation: Debt relief helps people with low incomes make ends meet and prevents them from falling into poverty. Stopping it would burden this group even more. We want to raise the threshold to 130% of the social minimum.

109164.explanation: Many people struggle due to high costs. That is why the income limit should be raised to 130% of the social minimum. This prevents people from just missing out and ensures subsistence security.

109166.explanation: Introducing a basic income leads to greater equality. The municipality will signal to the national government, which is responsible, that Amersfoort wants a pilot with a basic income.

109168.explanation: Parking at shopping centers remains paid (via parking disc zones). This ensures that parking spaces at shopping centers are used by shoppers. Parking for people with disabilities remains free.

109171.explanation: Amersfoort has a relatively high property tax rate, and the municipality's financial buffer is increasing. But there are also risks. We want to lower the property tax, but only if it is financially responsible.

109172.explanation: The dog tax will be abolished. Until that happens, proceeds will be used exclusively for dog facilities and will no longer be used for the general budget.

109174.explanation: Space in Amersfoort, Hoogland, and Hooglanderveen is scarce. Therefore, we want existing sites to be used smartly and efficiently, so we can also add sustainable manufacturing industry.

109176.explanation: Refugees must be able to move on so they can integrate and work. Housing is part of that.

109179.explanation: Every child must be able to obtain a swimming diploma, regardless of their parents' income.

109180.explanation: The Party for the Animals supports legalizing soft drugs. The number of coffee shops does not need to grow; there is room for seven coffee shops, which is sufficient for the size of the municipality.

109181.explanation: The municipality does not build homes itself; developers do. By requiring 40% social rental, many new construction projects do not get off the ground. That does not help. Therefore, we demand developers build two-thirds affordable housing and at least 35% social rental.

109187.explanation: The growth of car ownership in our city and villages blocks housing construction. Because the number of traffic movements on our roads determines how many homes we can build. The municipality will continue its current parking policy to make room for housing construction.

109188.explanation: Amersfoort deserves a central place for art and culture. The location of the current city hall could be a suitable place for a cultural center. This will only happen with broad social support. An alternative is housing construction.

109189.explanation: There is a significant lack of public natural swimming water in Amersfoort. The municipality will create a swimming pond in Hoogland-West.

109191.explanation: Amersfoort will not get an underpass of the railway at Barchman Wuytierslaan allowing cars through, so that the Western Access road will not be built later at the expense of nature.

109192.explanation: Those who sort their waste well pay less waste disposal charges. This makes it fair: less residual waste means lower costs. It encourages reuse and recycling.

109193.explanation: Amersfoort takes responsibility for generating sustainable energy. We only do this in places where people, animals, and nature are least affected, such as along highways and on industrial sites. We take extra measures to protect birds and bats.

109194.explanation: Due to the growth in the number of travelers, the stations must be expanded and improved. The municipality allocates funds and asks the national government and the region to contribute as well.

109195.explanation: Shelters for the homeless are necessary to ensure no one has to sleep outside against their will. The location on Bergstraat seems suitable. It is important that the municipality involves the neighborhood properly.

109135.explanation: There should mainly be fewer aldermen; currently, there are seven. We also seek better aldermen who have fewer conflicts with residents. The SP would find a city council made up of only men sad.

109136.explanation: Politics must follow the results of referendums with the policy requested in the referendum. The SP will always respect referendum outcomes, even if it holds a different opinion.

109139.explanation: Where possible, the SP supports free closing hours in the hospitality sector. Neighbors have the right to peace, safety, and sleep, so it is not possible everywhere. This requires clear agreements and mutual respect.

109141.explanation: The SP believes Amersfoort should shelter refugees, as every municipality should. Residents are involved and not taken by surprise in location choices.

109142.explanation: Wood burning may seem cozy, but for many people, it is anything but warm. It is unhealthy, polluting, and often a source of nuisance. Therefore, there is no place for wood burning in residential areas.

109149.explanation: The municipality should invest money in school buildings that need it, not automatically in all of them.

109154.explanation: Cameras sometimes capture crimes but do not prevent them. People now watching screens or checking footage afterward would be better placed in locations where it is unsafe.

109156.explanation: BOAs are not police officers. They are there to be visible in the neighborhood, to address people, help, and enforce with authority that stems from trust, not from armament. A baton changes that role. It makes the work harder, more dangerous, and more distant.

109159.explanation: Amersfoort does not give opportunities to slumlords. You buy a home to live in, not to speculate on or profit from.

109160.explanation: Offices stand empty all over the city. Dead square meters while people desperately search for an affordable home. That sums up the housing crisis: vacancy alongside housing shortage. This is not a natural phenomenon. It is a political choice. As far as the SP is concerned, offices should be converted wherever possible.

109161.explanation: At every place where temporary housing is built, permanent housing can also be built. Only then are we building a real solution to the housing shortage.

109163.explanation: The municipality must be generous towards residents living in poverty. We have seen in the childcare benefits affair that a government as a creditor can be a nightmare. We help our residents and do not harm them.

109164.explanation: In various municipalities where D'66, GroenLinks, and the Party for the Animals were in the executive board, there was a higher income threshold to help people than in Amersfoort. The SP does not understand why Amersfoort did not follow suit. It is so urgently needed, because more and more people are struggling to get by.

109166.explanation: The basic income is not suitable for a local trial.

109168.explanation: Parking fees should cover costs, so a parking rate is acceptable to the SP.

109171.explanation: The property tax (ozb) definitely does not need to be increased according to the SP. Especially the national government must take responsibility and provide municipalities with financially sound support again. Lowering the ozb before the national government contributes its fair share again is unfortunately not feasible. Not even if all prestige projects are canceled.

109172.explanation: More and more municipalities are abolishing the dog tax. Amersfoort can do this too; it is an arbitrary tax that mainly affects people with a small income.

109174.explanation: Space is under pressure in Amersfoort. Housing is scarce, greenery is disappearing, and the livability of neighborhoods is deteriorating. It is therefore not logical to blindly allow industrial estates to expand, as if space is infinite.

109176.explanation: Anyone with an urgent reason to be allowed to have housing should not be set against another with the same need. We must stop the sale of social rental homes by Alliantie and Portaal. Then everyone who needs a home can find one, including refugees.

109179.explanation: A swimming diploma should not depend on the choices or financial means of parents. School swimming is the way to guarantee that all children don’t have to fear water and learn to swim.

109180.explanation: Only the mayor is responsible for this.

109181.explanation: In the past 25 years, the city has grown by about 20,000 homes, but in that same period only about 500 social rental homes have been added. That is a disgraceful ratio. Amersfoort residents long for affordable rental homes; we cannot build enough of them.

109187.explanation: We will overturn the referendum and look for solutions at neighborhood level with the support of local residents for a fair parking policy.

109188.explanation: The quarter-billion-euro Culture Palace will not be built. We invest normal amounts that help FLUOR, the Flint, and the Onze Lieve Vrouwentheater. Housing can be built well at the city hall location.

109189.explanation: Amersfoort must invest in an attractive forest pool; we do not need a prestige pond dug somewhere.

109191.explanation: The SP opposes a car tunnel at Barchman Wuytierslaan because of the costs, the devastation of nature, and because it contributes little to traffic flow.

109192.explanation: Paying for waste leads to littering. We do not want Napoli on the Eem where garbage bags are stacked next to the waste containers.

109193.explanation: Wind turbines do not belong in urban areas. Amersfoort has many roofs suitable for solar panels; Amersfoort can do something about that.

109194.explanation: Let the NS invest money in renovating the stations. Then Amersfoort can spend its money on Amersfoort matters. It has been strategically very unwise of alderman Bijlholt to be the first to say he is willing to open his wallet; then others keep their hands in their pockets.

109195.explanation: The SP considers the location at Bergstraat suitable for homeless shelter. It is good that a wealthy neighborhood is taking its responsibility in this.

109135.explanation: The municipality must continue to pursue active policies to promote equality and inclusion. Better Amersfoort will wholeheartedly support this policy.

109136.explanation: We support referenda as an option for residents to have more influence on policy. The voice of residents must not be ignored (as with the parking permit policy where 74% of residents’ votes were ignored).

109139.explanation: We agree with this although we did not include it in the election program.

109141.explanation: The municipality is obliged to comply with the national requirement to accommodate asylum seekers, but this must also be feasible. Rejected asylum seekers do not belong in this.

109142.explanation: We find a charcoal ban for wood stoves a bit too far, but consideration for people who are bothered by it during wind or improper wood use should be self-evident.

109149.explanation: We agree with this. We have no separate chapter on education because many responsibilities lie with the national government and not the municipality, so we have little influence as council members.

109154.explanation: Camera surveillance is acceptable for structural problems, such as in Park Schothorst where incidents occur regularly.

109156.explanation: We want more BOAs in the city.

109159.explanation: On the contrary, we want Amersfoort residents to have priority for rental and owner-occupied homes. This also applies to young people returning after their studies and to elderly people moving to an apartment. This way families can live close together. That is pleasant for young and old.

109160.explanation: Owners of buildings that stand empty for too long without reason should be fined. Converting offices should be made easier. We would even like the municipality itself to buy land to build starter homes.

109161.explanation: We must be creative with the space that already exists. Splitting houses, room rentals, and converting offices should also be made easier. High-rise buildings are possible but the surroundings must be considered and livability must improve.

109163.explanation: For many households, local taxes are an extra financial burden. We want to slow the increase and reduce where possible.

109164.explanation: Better Amersfoort chooses investments that truly help residents, such as affordable housing, good care, and strong neighborhoods. This is also such a measure.

109166.explanation: Better Amersfoort chooses investments that truly help residents, such as affordable housing, good care, and strong neighborhoods. This is also such a measure.

109168.explanation: Paid parking may only be introduced if the majority of the neighborhood’s residents agree. Parking must not become a cash cow.

109171.explanation: We do not want too many unclear reserves lying dormant; we would rather see this money returned to residents themselves. We submitted a proposal to reduce the ozb by 100 euros for everyone; unfortunately, this did not pass.

109172.explanation: The dog tax should be abolished if it disappears into the general fund but may remain if the money is actually used for maintenance of dog walking areas or emptying dog waste bins.

109174.explanation: We do not agree with this.

109179.explanation: Everyone in Amersfoort should be able to engage in sports and physical activity. This is beneficial for both physical and mental health.

109180.explanation: We do not agree with this.

109181.explanation: 29% is the average percentage of social rental housing in the total housing stock in Amersfoort. If 40% is maintained for new construction, we might perhaps reach 30%. We would like to aim for an average of 35%. There are too few affordable rental homes; the waiting list is far too long!

109187.explanation: Paid parking may only be introduced if a majority of the residents in a neighborhood agree with it. Parking should not become a cash cow.

109188.explanation: This spot near the city center could be better used to create housing for young people and seniors.

109189.explanation: We agree with this. We did not specifically mention it in our election program, but we consider nature, greenery, and recreation important, and this is a good combination.

109191.explanation: This is not in our election program, but a car tunnel is too expensive. We would rather spend that money on affordable housing and facilities.

109192.explanation: We do not support paying for residual waste based on weight (Diftar). We expect many problems from the resulting dumping of waste in the living environment and extra pollution of the PMD, paper, and GFT waste streams. Waste processing must not become more expensive for residents.

109193.explanation: We are not against wind energy, but residents must be able to participate in the decision-making and be financially compensated. Also, safety is crucial. Therefore, we oppose a wind turbine on the Isselt.

109194.explanation: Better Amersfoort wants better public transport policy. Public transport must be made more attractive as an alternative to the car.

109135.explanation: Quality and expertise must be leading, not quotas. Appointments must be made based on merit.

109136.explanation: Residents must be able to express their opinions directly on important matters. Democracy requires engagement.

109139.explanation: Entrepreneurs must have the freedom to set their own opening hours, within reasonable frameworks of public order and livability.

109141.explanation: Housing for rejected asylum seekers is primarily a national responsibility. The municipality must monitor its capacity.

109142.explanation: Freedom must not come at the expense of health. Nuisance and air pollution require clear rules.

109149.explanation: Good, safe, and healthy school buildings are essential. Investing in educational housing is investing in Amersfoort’s future.

109154.explanation: Targeted camera surveillance can reduce social insecurity, provided privacy regulations are respected.

109156.explanation: Municipal enforcement officers (BOAs) must be able to do their work safely. Additional powers, such as carrying a baton, may be appropriate with increasing aggression.

109159.explanation: Speculation and property buying for rental drive prices up. Owner-occupied homes should primarily be intended for personal living to give first-time buyers more opportunities.

109160.explanation: Vacant office buildings must be transformed. Municipal support can help accelerate housing construction.

109161.explanation: Temporary housing can quickly relieve pressure on the housing market.

109163.explanation: Debt forgiveness helps the most vulnerable. Abolishing it would increase poverty.

109164.explanation: Support must reach people who just fall outside schemes. However, it must be done financially responsibly and fit within the budget.

109166.explanation: The focus should be on work, activation, and targeted support, not on unfocused income models.

109168.explanation: Free or affordable parking supports local entrepreneurs and keeps shopping areas accessible.

109171.explanation: The municipality must handle tax money carefully. Where possible, taxes should go down or at least not increase. Savings and prioritizing come first.

109172.explanation: Dog tax is unfair and often not cost-covering. Abolishing it fits with reducing the tax burden.

109174.explanation: Space for entrepreneurship and employment is important for the local economy.

109176.explanation: Giving priority increases the waiting time for other home seekers. Equal treatment is the starting point.

109179.explanation: Swimming skills are a basic safety measure. Municipal support is justified.

109180.explanation: Expansion is not necessary and could increase nuisance.

109181.explanation: The waiting time is unacceptable. At least 40% social rental in new construction is needed to address the housing shortage, alongside transformation and better housing flow.

109187.explanation: Parking policy must not be a means to generate profit. In many neighborhoods, parking should become affordable or free again unless demonstrated nuisance requires otherwise. Accessibility for residents is paramount.

109188.explanation: Housing construction has priority over large-scale cultural projects at this location.

109189.explanation: Recreation is important, but benefits, costs, and location must be carefully weighed. No prestige projects without clear added value.

109191.explanation: Accessibility and traffic safety are important. A tunnel can improve traffic flow and reduce dangerous situations.

109192.explanation: Stimulating waste reduction is good, but the system must be fair, feasible, and not cause dumping.

109193.explanation: Large-scale wind turbines within the municipality do not fit with livability and support.

109194.explanation: Stations are the city's calling cards. Investing in quality and safety is logical.

109195.explanation: Housing is necessary, but location choices must be made carefully with attention to safety and support.

109135.explanation: Aldermen should be chosen based on qualities, not on gender. We choose someone who is good for the position.

109136.explanation: Amersfoort for Freedom stands for direct democracy; the voice of the residents must truly be heard! The council must decide to adhere to the result of a referendum.

109139.explanation: Let the entrepreneur do business. It is always in the entrepreneur's interest and responsibility to maintain a good relationship with their surroundings.

109141.explanation: When an asylum seeker is rejected, they are expected to return to their country of origin. If they do not, they are illegal in the Netherlands. Illegal status is not rewarded; rules apply to everyone.

109142.explanation: The wood-burning ban is a good example of the patronizing policy in Amersfoort. Enforcement officers (BOAs) are deployed for this while they should be focused on tackling crime, not harassing citizens.

109149.explanation: Amersfoort for Freedom is critical of government spending, but for tasks that are core responsibilities of the municipality, the municipality must perform them as well as possible. Maintenance of school buildings is also a core municipal task.

109154.explanation: Amersfoort for Freedom opposes the introduction and expansion of the Smart City concept, which involves placing more and more security cameras in public spaces. Cameras can be useful in certain locations, but residents' privacy must always come first. We prefer visible police presence on the streets.

109156.explanation: Use of force is a big step for enforcement officers (BOAs); only the police are allowed to use force. They are extensively trained for this. When others step back, the police step forward. This cannot be expected from BOAs.

109159.explanation: Amersfoort for Freedom supports private property ownership. Legislation in this area goes too far. We cannot make rules about this because there can be many reasons why you cannot live in your own home (world trip, love, etc.).

109160.explanation: Amersfoort for Freedom supports converting offices into homes, where allowed by the zoning plan. This is easily possible in the current market without subsidies.

109161.explanation: There is high demand from students, starters, and others for this temporary housing. The municipality must create space for it. Because you cannot build this small housing without subsidies, the municipality should provide extra support.

109163.explanation: Municipal taxes such as sewer charges and waste fees are levied to pay for certain services, including for people with low incomes. We believe no distinctions should be made to keep taxes low for everyone.

109164.explanation: Amersfoort already has a relatively high income threshold for poverty assistance. If the municipality raises the income threshold again, lower middle-income groups will also fall below the poverty line.

109166.explanation: Amersfoort for Freedom opposes introducing a basic income. We prefer tax relief for our residents.

109168.explanation: Shopping areas must remain freely accessible. Paid parking should be abolished. If residents request it, a blue zone parking system can be introduced.

109171.explanation: We want to lower many taxes, including property tax. Amersfoort for Freedom aims to achieve this through much more efficient governance and fewer subsidies. This will lead to lower financial burdens for residents.

109172.explanation: We want to abolish the dog tax. The dog tax discriminates against a certain type of pet. Amersfoort for Freedom wants to fund this by much more efficient governance and fewer subsidies.

109174.explanation: Amersfoort has already allowed many business parks to lapse in the past. Its beautiful central position in the Netherlands actually offers opportunities for Amersfoort to attract more companies in the future if the market allows.

109176.explanation: Amersfoort for Freedom does not want priority for refugees with residence permits, so that people with existing ties to Amersfoort have a better chance at housing.

109179.explanation: Amersfoort for Freedom does not consider organizing school swimming a municipal task.

109180.explanation: Under strict conditions outside residential areas, this may be permitted. The influence of residents must weigh heavily when determining these locations.

109181.explanation: Amersfoort has now comfortably met the national norm with 35%. There is actually a need for affordable private rentals and affordable owner-occupied homes.

109187.explanation: The municipality must honor the referendum result and reverse parking policies in areas without problems. Parking policies should only be applied affordably where direct neighbors experience issues.

109188.explanation: The cultural center (culture temple) will cost around €200 million! Amersfoort must stop prestige projects like this cultural center and other expensive non-essential projects to save funds for the residents. This location is better suited for new housing.

109189.explanation: There are plenty of water recreation areas in the region (Henschotermeer swimming lake, Bosbad, etc.). It is not the municipality’s task to create its own swimming lake.

109191.explanation: A serious solution must be sought for the traffic flow at Barchman-Wuytierslaan. This could be done with a bridge, which is much cheaper than a tunnel in a difficult location.

109192.explanation: Punishing residents by making them pay extra for disposing of waste backfires and causes people to dump their waste illegally. This is undesirable and creates even more problems.

109193.explanation: We stand for realistic environmental policy, not imposed climate policies. Wind turbines are a solution that can cause health damage due to noise and shadow flicker. We will do everything to prevent wind turbines close to our city.

109194.explanation: The plan to renovate the stations will cost more than €300 million and is irresponsible. There is little wrong with the current station and Schothorst station.

109195.explanation: Additional shelter for the homeless is necessary, but this location is unsuitable according to us and the neighbors. Participation clearly demonstrated this.

109135.explanation: BBB looks at quality, not quotas. The council must consist of the best people for Amersfoort, with experience, common sense, and support. Equal opportunities for all, but no mandatory male-female distribution.

109136.explanation: BBB Amersfoort truly wants to involve residents in important decisions. For major and impactful topics, residents must be able to voice their opinion via a referendum. The city's voice counts, not just that of city hall.

109139.explanation: BBB Amersfoort stands for trust and fewer rules. Entrepreneurs know best what works for their business and neighborhood. Let cafés and restaurants set their opening hours within clear and reasonable agreements.

109141.explanation: BBB Amersfoort stands for clarity and justice. Those without residency rights do not belong in municipal shelters permanently. We focus on return and national responsibility, with attention to safety and the city's capacity.

109142.explanation: The government should not interfere too much in everything. Neighbors should resolve issues among themselves.

109149.explanation: BBB Amersfoort considers good education essential. Safe, healthy, and future-proof school buildings are a basic provision. Investing in schools is investing in our children and Amersfoort’s future.

109154.explanation: BBB Amersfoort wants real safety, not symbolic measures. Camera surveillance is often not the solution. Safety requires common sense, addressing nuisance and crime, and visible enforcement in public spaces.

109156.explanation: BBB Amersfoort wants enforcement officers (BOAs) to be able to do their work safely. When enforcing on the street, they must have appropriate tools. A baton can contribute to their safety and authority, provided it is well-trained and responsibly used.

109159.explanation: BBB Amersfoort believes the government should not unnecessarily interfere with the housing market. Rigid obligations do not help. We prefer focusing on more construction and better flow rather than extra rules.

109160.explanation: BBB Amersfoort supports converting vacant offices into housing but subsidies should not be automatic. The municipality should mainly cooperate, speed up procedures, and remove obstacles, not routinely add funding.

109161.explanation: BBB Amersfoort recognizes that students need housing, but temporary container homes are not a structural solution. The municipality should focus on appropriate, sustainable housing and careful use of public funds.

109163.explanation: BBB Amersfoort believes that people with a low income should not be further burdened. Exemption from municipal taxes is necessary to keep basic expenses affordable and to prevent financial problems from escalating.

109164.explanation: BBB Amersfoort recognizes that people just above the current income threshold also struggle to make ends meet. By raising the income limit, we prevent residents from falling through the cracks and provide timely support.

109166.explanation: BBB Amersfoort chooses rewarding work and targeted support. A basic income is expensive, uncertain in effect, and removes incentives to participate. We prefer to focus on tailored solutions, guidance to employment, and targeted poverty reduction.

109168.explanation: BBB Amersfoort wants to keep local shops accessible. Free parking at shopping centers supports entrepreneurs and makes shopping accessible for everyone. Parking policies should enhance livability, not hinder it.

109171.explanation: BBB Amersfoort wants to keep housing and entrepreneurship affordable. Lowering the property tax reduces the financial burden for residents and entrepreneurs. Raising taxes is not an end in itself; careful and responsible management of public funds is.

109172.explanation: BBB Amersfoort believes the dog tax is outdated. Dog owners receive no direct benefit from it. Abolishing it is fairer and fits with limiting unnecessary local charges.

109174.explanation: BBB Amersfoort wants to create space for entrepreneurship and employment. Expansion of business parks is possible, provided it is integrated carefully, easily accessible, and balanced with housing, nature, and livability.

109176.explanation: BBB Amersfoort believes social rental housing should first be intended for residents with local ties. Priority for status holders increases pressure on the housing market and is unfair to those who have been waiting a long time for housing.

109179.explanation: BBB Amersfoort finds school swimming important for children's safety. Every child must learn to swim, regardless of the parents’ financial situation. Municipal support prevents unequal opportunities and increases swimming safety.

109180.explanation: BBB Amersfoort sees no need for new coffeeshops. They can increase nuisance and safety risks. The municipality should focus on peace, livability, and enforcement, not on expanding soft drug sales.

109181.explanation: BBB Amersfoort wants a balanced housing mix. More social rental homes are needed, but a fixed percentage for the entire city does not work everywhere. Tailored solutions per neighborhood, fitting needs, livability, and housing flow.

109187.explanation: BBB Amersfoort wants an accessible city. Free parking lowers barriers for residents and visitors and supports local amenities. Parking policy should serve livability, not filling the municipal coffers.

109188.explanation: BBB Amersfoort wants to handle this location carefully. The future use of the current city hall must fit the city, be financially feasible, and widely supported by residents. This requires an open assessment.

109189.explanation: BBB Amersfoort wants residents to relax and recreate close to home. A local swimming pond offers cooling, relaxation, and meeting opportunities for young and old and contributes to livability and health, provided it is safe and well integrated.

109191.explanation: BBB Amersfoort wants safe and smooth traffic flow. A car tunnel at Barchman Wuytierslaan can reduce traffic jams and unsafe situations. But only if it is technically feasible and fits within livability and the environment.

109192.explanation: BBB Amersfoort sees a link between waste amount and costs as logical. At the same time, we do not want a complicated or bureaucratic system. Waste policy must remain simple, fair, and feasible.

109193.explanation: BBB Amersfoort does not want wind turbines in the city. They have a large impact on the landscape and livability. Sustainability must be feasible and supported: prioritize solar panels on roofs and other suitable solutions first.

109194.explanation: BBB Amersfoort wants safe, accessible, and attractive stations. Investing in Amersfoort Central and Schothorst improves accessibility, social safety, and the city’s appearance, in cooperation with partners and with attention to efficiency.

109195.explanation: BBB Amersfoort believes good shelter for the homeless is necessary, but the location must suit safety, support, and accessibility. Whether the Bergstraat location fits requires careful consideration with the neighborhood and involved organizations.

109135.explanation: We believe the most suitable candidate should be selected, regardless of gender, orientation, religion, or political preference.

109136.explanation: This is already possible and we want to continue promoting it.

109139.explanation: To prevent nuisance and protect public order, we do not want hospitality entrepreneurs to fully determine their own opening hours. This must remain within reasonable limits.

109141.explanation: We do not support illegal residence.

109142.explanation: We do not want changes to the current policy.

109149.explanation: This is not necessary for every school building.

109154.explanation: In structurally unsafe locations, we want increased camera surveillance.

109156.explanation: We want a pilot to see if this has positive effects on the safety of enforcement officers.

109159.explanation: We want to protect starters in Amersfoort and, where possible, give them priority when buying a home.

109160.explanation: We want to support this to combat the housing crisis.

109161.explanation: We want to support this to combat the housing crisis.

109163.explanation: This financial support is essential and we want to continue providing it.

109164.explanation: In line with inflation and wage increases, we want to raise the income limit.

109166.explanation: We want to do this through a basic job pilot.

109168.explanation: We want to adjust the current parking policy so that parking becomes free again.

109171.explanation: This seems appealing to us; we want to apply it where possible.

109172.explanation: The money should be used for dog owners, but this is currently not happening. Therefore, we believe this tax should be abolished.

109174.explanation: We want to use the suitable locations for housing development.

109176.explanation: This is necessary to comply with Dutch law. After granting a residence permit, a home must be allocated within 10 weeks. Without priority, this is practically unfeasible, so we unfortunately must maintain it.

109179.explanation: Currently, this seems unfeasible to us, but in the future, we want to look into it.

109180.explanation: We do not want to encourage this, but neither do we want to categorically prohibit expansion.

109181.explanation: We want 35% social rental housing in Amersfoort. If this exceeds 35%, there will be too few homes available for Amersfoort residents who do not qualify for social housing.

109187.explanation: Parking should be free again throughout Amersfoort.

109189.explanation: This is already being realized at 't Hammetje in Amersfoort North.

109191.explanation: To promote safety, traffic flow, and nature.

109192.explanation: We are against diftar because it leads to more littering and higher costs.

109193.explanation: This causes too much nuisance. There are better ways to generate green electricity.

109194.explanation: As a result, people use public transport more quickly and leave their cars at home sooner.

109195.explanation: We want to look for another location for homeless shelter.

109135.explanation: KeiHart for Amersfoort stands for reliable governance that speaks frankly and stands by what it says. It listens to residents. A reflection is good, but we believe suitability follows from relevant work experience and personal qualities—not whether the official is male or female.

109136.explanation: KeiHart for Amersfoort wants a 'Citizen Chamber.' The Citizen Chamber is a model intended to ensure the government is there again for its residents. They can give their opinions on all subjects. As long as the Citizen Chamber does not exist, we will maintain the referendum ordinance and follow up on its results.

109139.explanation: KeiHart for Amersfoort believes that café and restaurant entrepreneurs should be able to decide for themselves, within certain frameworks, when they are open or closed. Naturally, in consultation with the neighborhood. Extended opening hours can help spread visits more evenly, which may lead to less nuisance.

109141.explanation: KeiHart for Amersfoort believes that rejected asylum seekers, after any appeal against that decision, should leave the country as soon as possible. By continuing to offer shelter, people stay longer here; given the limited capacity and pressure on social services, this is undesirable.

109142.explanation: KeiHart for Amersfoort wants to focus on smart solutions for better air quality, such as supporting the placement of wood smoke filters through the municipality. We thus refrain from enforcing the unmanageable wood burning ban and free up BOA capacity for other matters.

109149.explanation: KeiHart for Amersfoort believes money should be invested in buildings that need it most. A recently completed new building does not need improvements in the near future.

109154.explanation: KeiHart for Amersfoort prefers more police and BOAs visibly on the street but is open to camera surveillance if residents also support it. We want to apply camera surveillance and preventive searches more often in high-risk safety areas and in cases of (repeated) nuisance.

109156.explanation: KeiHart for Amersfoort believes that besides increasing the number of BOAs, they should also receive more resources. A baton is an example. Of course, we believe public order enforcers should only use their tools proportionally.

109159.explanation: KeiHart for Amersfoort wants (owner-occupied) housing primarily for people to live in themselves and would rather not see houses as speculative objects. Buying a home to rent out should remain possible but within certain limits.

109160.explanation: KeiHart for Amersfoort is not opposed to converting vacant offices into housing, but would like sufficient facilities for residents living there. The municipality can contribute to certain projects or conditions, but this should not be automatic as far as we are concerned.

109161.explanation: KeiHart for Amersfoort believes the municipality must ensure appropriate housing that meets demand and needs. If temporary container homes help students in the short term, then the municipality can contribute to the conditions.

109163.explanation: KeiHart for Amersfoort believes everyone should in principle contribute to the shared common costs we have. However, when people are fully dependent on government money, such as welfare, this is pointless: giving money only to ask it back as taxes.

109164.explanation: KeiHart for Amersfoort believes this should be determined nationally. That ensures every resident in every municipality is treated equally. It's unfair if differences arise within the Netherlands. Also, people might then strategically request assistance.

109166.explanation: KeiHart for Amersfoort does not support a basic income and opposes doing a pilot with it. When people need help, we support them temporarily, but help is a trampoline, not a hammock. The goal is work or social work as soon as possible.

109168.explanation: KeiHart for Amersfoort favors reinstating the blue parking disc around shopping areas where regulation is needed. Shopkeepers suffer directly from barriers such as paid parking, resulting in turnover loss and vacancies.

109171.explanation: KeiHart for Amersfoort believes the property tax (ozb) should not increase. Due to rising property values, revenues are already increasing. This might even make a reduction possible. We stand for healthy municipal finances and fair, low costs for residents.

109172.explanation: KeiHart for Amersfoort wants to abolish the dog tax. The pet tax contributes little to Amersfoort’s budget (0.11%) but mainly affects low-income residents. We stand for healthy municipal finances and fair, low costs for residents.

109174.explanation: KeiHart for Amersfoort recognizes a growing municipality also needs space for businesses and other facilities, often located on industrial estates. This is also good for the local labor market.

109176.explanation: KeiHart for Amersfoort wants to stop giving priority to status holders, as this is not legally required and displaces regular housing seekers in the market. Many (young) starters can barely enter now, waiting lists increase, and priority for status holders is unfair.

109179.explanation: KeiHart for Amersfoort sees that the municipality already does this and we want that to continue. Swimming skills are important to prevent drownings. Ideally, everyone learns at least the basics of swimming at school.

109180.explanation: KeiHart for Amersfoort is against more coffee shops and especially not in residential areas. Amersfoort currently has 7 coffee shops, which we find sufficient. Those that exist are mainly on industrial estates, which seems the best compromise.

109181.explanation: KeiHart for Amersfoort sees that too few social rental homes have been built in recent years. In 2012 (CBS), 32% of Amersfoort’s homes were social rental; now only 29%. We want more social rental homes, for example towards 35% of the total, but no fixed percentage per project.

109187.explanation: KeiHart for Amersfoort wants sufficient parking spaces. We oppose expanding paid and permit parking and want to reverse that expansion as quickly and as much as possible. Furthermore, we will abolish the day ticket and lower on-street parking rates.

109188.explanation: KeiHart for Amersfoort supports good distribution of cultural locations throughout our municipality. This spreads both the burdens and benefits. We do not want a €230 million culture palace at the current city hall site; we prefer housing there.

109189.explanation: KeiHart for Amersfoort advocates for a safe, freely accessible public swimming lake, for example at ’t Hammetje or another location in Amersfoort. In summer, people swim in unsafe places, both regarding water quality and ship traffic.
