109191.explanation: KeiHart for Amersfoort stands for good and safe traffic. We want to invest in infrastructure and traffic flow. When tunneling the Barchman Wuytierslaan, we want not only a tunnel for cyclists, buses, and emergency services but also for cars. If you do it: do it right!

109192.explanation: KeiHart for Amersfoort does not want to introduce diftar (differentiated rates) waste charges. Paying per bag of residual waste tends to encourage illegal dumping or contamination of waste streams. We want to investigate whether post-separation is a better and cheaper option for our waste.

109193.explanation: KeiHart for Amersfoort is not in favor of onshore wind energy. Therefore, we do not want wind turbines on the Isselt or in Hoogland-West. Near the Isselt wind turbine site are a chemical factory, sewage treatment plant, homes, offices, and a gas plant. This is unsafe.

109194.explanation: KeiHart for Amersfoort wants to invest in pleasant and safe outdoor spaces. Around Schothorst Station and Amersfoort Central, where the municipality is responsible, we want to ensure enough lighting, parking spaces, bike parking, and good accessibility.

109195.explanation: KeiHart for Amersfoort believes no one should have to sleep on the street in Amersfoort. We always place homeless shelters in consultation with the neighborhood. At Bergstraat, no consultation took place with residents; therefore, we oppose a shelter at this location.

109135.explanation: We believe the appointment of aldermen and the mayor should be based on the right qualities and skills. We do not support instituting a quota.

109136.explanation: The referendum is one of the most important tools for encouraging citizen participation, but its outcomes must be acted upon. Therefore, we want it to be possible to reject proposals through a corrective binding referendum.

109139.explanation: To keep Amersfoort livable, it is important to maintain clear regulations for opening and closing times. This way we regulate nuisance at certain times and ensure a pleasant living environment for residents.

109141.explanation: We focus on cooperation with the national government and institutions to develop central policies for rejected asylum seekers. If asylum seekers end up on the street, they will be taken in. This prevents people from sleeping on the streets or causing potential nuisance.

109142.explanation: With rising energy and gas costs, heating one's home with wood stoves or an open fireplace can be cheaper. This must remain possible. To prevent nuisance such as fine dust and odor, there are alternatives to a ban, such as installing filters on the stove pipe.

109149.explanation: All school buildings in Amersfoort must meet the standards of a good educational institution; our youth are our future and must be invested in. We will invest where improvements are most urgently needed, not in all school buildings.

109154.explanation: Only in certain places and no data collection if not necessary. The municipality is accountable. More camera surveillance in the city center, for example near nightlife venues. Emphasis on the safety of women. We are therefore in favor, with conditions and for good reasons.

109156.explanation: There is an increasing occurrence of excessive violence against enforcement officers (boa's) who address people about their behavior. Especially in unsafe situations, boa's must be able to act without fear. Teaching the discreet use of a baton is a solution.

109159.explanation: The municipality must act against resellers and large investment companies. However, it should remain possible for parents to buy or own an apartment or house for one of their children, or to rent out a home, for example.

109160.explanation: The housing crisis also requires adjustments to zoning plans. In Amersfoort, several office buildings are vacant in various locations and could be converted into housing.

109161.explanation: We prioritize permanent housing. In doing so, we are compelled to also invest in creative options, such as tiny houses, container homes, and splitting and adding floors to existing homes.

109163.explanation: In urgent cases, it must remain possible for the municipality to relieve residents of their debts to help them move forward.

109164.explanation: The causes of increasing poverty in Amersfoort must be examined. The municipality can better focus its efforts there to help and support people where necessary.

109171.explanation: The property tax (ozb) must not increase further, in any case.

109174.explanation: If there is room, there must be a good mix between residential space and businesses. We want existing plans to continue.

109176.explanation: To promote the integration of status holders and keep them off the streets, it is important to give them priority for social rental housing. The national inflow must be addressed; no longer granting priority to status holders would only treat symptoms.

109179.explanation: The Netherlands is a water country, and in Amersfoort, there is plenty of water around us. The youth must be prepared for this. Now swimming lessons are more expensive and even unaffordable for some; we believe it must again become part of the fixed curriculum in schools.

109180.explanation: We want to give the market and entrepreneurs as much free rein as possible. If there is demand for more coffee shops, the municipality should not prohibit that. This must take into account the space required for the coffee shop and potential nuisance.

109181.explanation: We aim for 33 percent social rental, 33 percent private rental, and 33 percent owner-occupied housing in new construction projects. We focus on mixed new construction where as many Amersfoort residents as possible can find suitable housing.

109187.explanation: The proposed plan to implement paid parking in all neighborhoods in Amersfoort is, in our view, not the right way. Paid parking may be necessary in neighborhoods with parking problems. In neighborhoods without parking issues, there will be no paid parking.

109189.explanation: Space in Amersfoort is limited. A new swimming lake is not a priority. The Amersfoort Forest Pool already offers swimming facilities.

109192.explanation: A fixed municipal tax for waste processing ensures that people will not avoid disposing of waste. This prevents illegal dumping and unnecessary use of public trash bins.

109193.explanation: There is no place for wind turbines in Amersfoort. They would cause too much nuisance for residents and forest areas.

109195.explanation: Homeless shelters must be given the space they need. No one should be forced to sleep on the streets. Therefore, a new location for homeless shelter must be considered. Its location must be determined in close consultation with neighbors.

109135.explanation: Politics must reflect society. That also applies to the executive board. Because men are still overrepresented, it is important to strive for an equal distribution. That way, the city government sets a good example.

109136.explanation: We support participation but not referendums. Referendums make complex choices black-and-white and increase polarization. Therefore, Volt prefers citizens' assemblies, where randomly selected residents study issues and jointly develop supported solutions. Citizens' assemblies lead to better decisions and more connection.

109139.explanation: A vibrant city is alive in the evening and night. That includes space for hospitality and nightlife culture. Volt wants to give entrepreneurs room to improve nightlife. At the same time, we take nuisance seriously and make clear agreements with neighbors.

109141.explanation: Volt stands for humane treatment and good guidance for returnees. Decisions about rejected asylum seekers and their shelter must be carefully carried out, but this is a task of the national government.

109142.explanation: Volt values clean air, especially in residential areas. Fireplaces and wood stoves cause a lot of fine dust and health complaints, particularly among children and the elderly. 'Always allowing' ignores this. That is why we agree that the use of fireplaces and stoves should sometimes be prohibited.

109149.explanation: We believe school buildings must be safe, healthy, and sustainable, but priorities must be set. Volt wants to first improve those schools with the most urgent maintenance needs and where sustainability measures yield the most benefits.

109154.explanation: Cameras increase the chance of catching offenders and contribute to a feeling of safety, though they do not prevent everything. Therefore, we want to deploy cameras targeted at places where they are truly needed, as a complement to prevention and enforcement.

109156.explanation: We want boa’s to do their work safely. However, arming them does not automatically fit their role as approachable enforcers in the neighborhood because it can increase the chance of escalation. Therefore, Volt believes baton use should depend on situations, timing, and locations.

109159.explanation: Volt believes homes are meant for living in, not for making money. A mandatory owner-occupancy rule prevents homes from being bought up for profit while first-time buyers have no chance. This keeps homes available for people who genuinely want to live here.

109160.explanation: Converting vacant offices is faster than new construction and prevents wasting space. With targeted subsidies, the municipality can enable this conversion. It is also important to invest in good facilities so that genuinely new living space is created.

109161.explanation: Volt wants permanent, affordable housing for students, starters, and young professionals. Temporary housing is not a real solution to the housing shortage because it postpones the problem and later creates uncertainty.

109163.explanation: Debt forgiveness prevents small amounts from turning into large debts and provides peace and clarity. Problem debts also cost the government an enormous amount of money. Therefore, we want to actively tackle problematic debts, for example by buying them and paying them off.

109164.explanation: Many people earn just too much for assistance but still struggle to make ends meet. This causes stress and uncertainty. By raising the income limit, we can also help this group in time and prevent future costs.

109166.explanation: Volt supports a basic income but opposes a local pilot. Previous pilots have often been limited. Therefore, we prefer a national pilot. In Amersfoort, we focus on activating social assistance and one simple poverty scheme. This way, we still provide more trust and room.

109168.explanation: Space in the city is scarce. We believe that scarce space must be used smartly and forward-looking for greenery, cycling, and safe walking routes. This makes shopping centers accessible in a sustainable and healthy way.

109171.explanation: We understand that many people watch their housing costs. But lowering the property tax (ozb) means less money for care, safety, greenery, and facilities in neighborhoods. Therefore, we choose to invest in the future, the welfare of Amersfoort residents, and a financially healthy budget.

109172.explanation: No one likes paying taxes, but the dog tax contributes to a clean and safe public space. This money is used for facilities, enforcement, and cleaning up dog waste.

109174.explanation: We want to transform the Amersfoort economy with a future focus. This requires space. More room must be made for businesses with sustainable and circular business models, such as recycling, reuse, repair, and clean technology. Together with businesses, we determine what is needed and where this can take place.

109176.explanation: We believe that people who are allowed to stay should be able to rebuild their lives as quickly as possible. A home is the foundation for this. Preferably, we give status holders a place in or near the neighborhood where they are already being accommodated. This ensures faster integration, less uncertainty, and more tranquility in the neighborhood.

109179.explanation: We believe every child should be able to grow up safely in a country with water. School swimming increases swimming skills and reduces risks, especially for children who get less of this at home. By investing in this, we ensure safety and equal opportunities.

109180.explanation: Amersfoort is an open and mature city, and that includes a realistic approach to soft drugs. Coffeeshops provide fixed locations, clear rules, and supervision for the sale of weed and hash. This is safe for users and the environment around the coffeeshop.

109181.explanation: We want many more affordable homes for people who are currently stuck. Therefore, as far as Volt is concerned, at least two-thirds of new construction must be affordable, and at least 30% of that social rental housing. What is needed varies per neighborhood and location.

109187.explanation: Many Amersfoort residents just want to park their car without hassle. But we also notice how nice and important it is when streets are quieter and there is space for walking, cycling, or playing outside. That is why we want affordable parking, with good and fair alternatives to the car.

109188.explanation: We want this place to be for and by the people of Amersfoort. Not a grand cultural palace competing with Utrecht or Leusden, but an open and accessible place where the city comes together. A cultural center that grows along: for local creators, music, debate, youth, neighborhood initiatives, and social gatherings.

109189.explanation: On warm days, people want to be able to cool off and relax close to home without having to pay for it. A swimming pond provides space for recreation, meeting, and health, for young and old. Wouldn't it be nice, for example, to be able to take a dip in the Eem?

109191.explanation: With a growing city, good accessibility is very important. Therefore, a good western route is needed so that traffic can flow better and residential neighborhoods are relieved. It is important that this does not come at the expense of much greenery and that this is properly compensated nearby in another way.

109192.explanation: We think it is fair that those who produce little waste also pay less. Currently, everyone often pays the same regardless of how much waste they throw away. By paying per waste bag, we reward people who separate waste well and use resources consciously. Less waste means lower costs.

109193.explanation: We believe Amersfoort must take responsibility for generating sustainable energy. Wind turbines can play a role in this. We will do this carefully, with attention to health, neighbors, and nature.

109194.explanation: Good stations are important to us. We want to invest in public space around stations, bicycle facilities, and good connections in the neighborhood. It is the responsibility of NS and ProRail to keep stations safe, clean, and accessible themselves.

109195.explanation: No one should have to sleep on the street. Homeless shelters are needed to provide people with rest and perspective. But nearby residents were involved too late in the location choice. We therefore understand the considerable resistance and concerns. Humanity applies to both the homeless and the neighborhood where they live.
